title: Temple of the Oracle (Aghurmi)
description: Famous temple dedicated to Amun, visited by Alexander the Great to confirm his divinity. Built on the acropolis of Aghurmi.

title: Temple of Umm Ubayd
description: Ruins of a temple dedicated to Amun, located near the Oracle Temple. Known for its inscribed wall depicting Wenamun.

title: Gebel al-Mawta (Mountain of the Dead)
description: A conical hill containing rock-cut tombs from the Pharaonic, Ptolemaic, and Roman periods. Contains several important painted tombs.

title: Tomb of Si Amun
description: Calculated as the most beautiful tomb in the Western Desert. Si Amun was likely a wealthy Greek landowner living in Siwa who adopted Egyptian customs.

title: Tomb of Mesu-Isis
description: Contains depictions of Osiris and Isis. The owner's name means 'Born of Isis'.

title: Tomb of Niperpathot
description: One of the oldest tombs in Siwa, belonging to the 'Prophet of Osiris' and scribe of divine documents.

title: Tomb of the Crocodile
description: Named after a painting of a yellow crocodile. The tomb has three chambers and dates to the late Ptolemaic period.

title: Shali Fortress (Old Shali)
description: The 13th-century organic mud-brick fortress located in the center of Siwa town. Built from 'kershef' (salt-impregnated mud).

title: Cleopatra’s Bath (Spring of the Sun)
description: A popular natural spring where the water is warm in winter and cool in summer. Not actually connected to Queen Cleopatra historically, but a key site.

title: Dakrur Mountain
description: Site of the annual Siyaha festival. Known for its healing hot sand baths and limestone caves.

title: Archaeological Site of Marina El Alamein (Leucaspis)
description: An extensive Greco-Roman harbor city discovered in the 1980s. Contains patrician houses, a forum, and a basilica.

title: Roman Baths at Marina
description: Well-preserved public bath complex featuring hypocaust heating systems and mosaics.

title: House H9 & H10 (Marina)
description: Examples of affluent dwelling houses with peristyle courtyards and porticos found in the ancient city.

title: Marina Necropolis
description: A vast cemetery with varied tomb types including pillar tombs and pyramid tombs.

title: Temple of Ramesses II (Zawiyyat Umm El Rakham)
description: A fortress-town built by Ramesses II to defend Egypt's western border from Libyan tribes. Contains temple ruins and chapels.

title: Cleopatra’s Bath (Matrouh)
description: A rock formation in the sea with a natural pool, believed to be used by Cleopatra and Marc Antony.

title: Rommel’s Cave Museum
description: A U-shaped cave in the rock used by Erwin Rommel as headquarters during WWII. Now a military museum.

title: Ruins of the Coptic Chapel
description: Early Coptic Christian ruins located near the corniche, indicating the spread of Christianity in the region.

title: Commonwealth War Cemetery (El Alamein)
description: Resting place for thousands of Allied soldiers who died in the Western Desert campaigns.

title: El Alamein War Museum
description: Museum documenting the North African battles with outdoor displays of tanks and artillery.

title: German War Memorial
description: An ossuary built in the style of a fortress, overlooking the sea, holding the remains of German soldiers.

title: Italian War Memorial
description: A white marble tower dedicated to Italian soldiers. The interior leads to the top for a view of the battlefield.

title: Qara Oasis (Gara)
description: A tiny, isolated oasis on the edge of the Qattara Depression. Features a ruined fortress-village atop a mushroom rock.

title: Mersa Matruh Naval Base Ruins
description: Underwater remains of ancient breakwaters and jetties from the Roman port of Paraetonium.
